description: Use PyMOL's internal builder to generate an ideal alpha-helical model of the Magainin-2 peptide sequence: KWKLFKKIGIGKFLHSAKKF. Do not download any PDB files. Color the structure to highlight its amphipathic nature: color all hydrophobic residues (W, L, F, I, G, A) yellow or orange, color all cationic/basic residues (K, H) blue, and color the remaining polar residues (S) green. Measure the end-to-end length of the helix (distance between the C-alpha atom of residue 1 and the C-alpha atom of residue 20). Orient the helix to display its distinct hydrophobic and hydrophilic faces. Save a publication-quality ray-traced image of the colored helix to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/magainin_amphipathic.png. Export the generated 3D coordinates as a standard PDB file to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/magainin_ideal.pdb. Write a plain-text report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/peptide_report.txt containing: the primary sequence used, the measured end-to-end CA-CA distance in Angstroms, and the total count of basic/cationic residues (Lys + His) in the sequence.
